Cryptotaenia qinghao, also known as Qinghao, is a traditional Chinese medicinal plant that belongs to the Apiaceae family. The health benefits of Qinghao include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its ability to repel insects. The bioactive constituents of Qinghao include artemisinin, a sesquiterpene lactone, as well as other compounds such as artemisinic acid and daidzein. The most common herbal preparations made with Qinghao include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
